title: Remedial Chaos Theory

jokes.text: ♪Troy and Abed's new apartment! ♪
jokes.setup: Characters entering apartment for first time
jokes.punchline: Singing their signature jingle for the new location
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only these two would sing this. Economy of words, instant recognition for fans.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We're moving in, yeah, yeah!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: New place, new adventures!

dialogue.speaker: Troy and Abed
dialogue.line: Troy and Abed in the mooooorning!

jokes.text: avoid touchy topics like the negro problem. The book was written in the '40s.
jokes.setup: Reading from a book on party hosting etiquette
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning outdated racist terminology with matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works because of the matter-of-fact delivery and historical context explanation. Risky but well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: According to this book on party hosting etiquette, you should never use outdated racist terminology.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What if you just casually mention it with a matter-of-fact explanation?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's still racist, Troy.

jokes.text: No, it doesn't. [about time flying when baking]
jokes.setup: Shirley saying 'Time flies when I'm baking'
jokes.punchline: Flat contradiction of the cliché
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan rejection of pleasantries. Functional but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Time flies when I'm baking.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No it doesn't. You've been standing there for four hours.

jokes.text: scale model of the rolling Boulder scene from raiders... With actual rolling Boulder. Adiós, Sapito.
jokes.setup: Showing off apartment features to guests
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Indiana Jones recreation that actually works, with Spanish farewell to toy figure
jokes.explanation: Pure Abed - obsessive pop culture recreation with perfect character integration. Visual comedy gold.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Check this out. I installed a pressure-sensitive floor tile that triggers a rolling boulder mechanism.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: And here's the golden idol—perfectly balanced so it triggers the trap when you remove it.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Watch this.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Adiós, mi amor.

jokes.text: Super cool. And sexy. Super sexy cool. Overselling it.
jokes.setup: Trying to compliment Abed's Boulder model
jokes.punchline: Escalating praise followed by immediate meta-commentary calling out the overselling
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the awkwardness of forced compliments. Well-structured escalation and deflation.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Abed, this Boulder model is incredible! The detail work is just... I mean, wow!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Seriously, it's like you captured the exact essence of Boulder. Every rock, every shadow...

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It's basically a work of art. Museum-quality. Like, I wouldn't be surprised if someone offered you money for this right now.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You guys realize you're doing that thing where you oversell something to make the person feel good, and then you immediately point it out, which somehow makes it worse?

jokes.text: You didn't have some of the ingredients, so I used ketchup and cream cheese. We ordered real pizza.
jokes.setup: Shirley made pizza to compete with ordered pizza
jokes.punchline: Absurd ingredient substitutions followed by blunt rejection
jokes.explanation: Classic Shirley trying too hard to help. The ingredient substitutions are absurd enough to be funny.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I made a pizza with cauliflower crust, nutritional yeast instead of cheese, and these are sun-dried tomatoes I prepared myself.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's great, Shirley.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Well? Try it!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Bienvenido de la Casa Chez Trobed.
jokes.setup: Greeting Jeff at the door
jokes.punchline: Mixing Spanish and French with their combined names
jokes.explanation: Abed's linguistic playfulness and apartment portmanteau. Character-specific humor.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Bonjour, Jeff. Je m'appelle Abed-ieu.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's French and Spanish combined. Like our names. Abed-ieu. Get it? A-bed and adieu.

jokes.text: Indiana Jones and the apartment of perpetual virginity.
jokes.setup: Seeing Abed's Indiana Jones display
jokes.punchline: Casting it as evidence of sexual inexperience in Indiana Jones title format
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure - uses Indiana Jones title format to mock the very thing being referenced. Perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wow, Abed. This is a lot of Indiana Jones stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I know. I'm a fan.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, I can see that. You know what this reminds me of? 'Indiana Jones and the Temple of Virgin.'

jokes.text: Chop busted, fellow adult, chop busted.
jokes.setup: Responding to Jeff's insult
jokes.punchline: Overly formal acknowledgment of being insulted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy response - trying to be mature but in a childlike way. 'Fellow adult' is the key phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I acknowledge receipt of your insult and have filed it away for future reference.

jokes.text: Didn't you used to live in a mansion with me? Yeah, but this is more my speed and century.
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning Pierce about living standards
jokes.punchline: Pierce dismissing his own mansion as too old-fashioned
jokes.explanation: Pierce's age joke at his own expense. 'Speed and century' is a nice turn of phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, your mansion is incredible. How can you possibly complain about it?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Jeff, it was built in 1987. That's basically a nursing home for real estate.

jokes.text: I've hardly missed you at all since I had you removed from my portraits.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about missing Jeff
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning having Jeff erased from family photos
jokes.explanation: Delightfully petty and specific to Pierce's character. The casualness makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yeah, I had him erased from all my family photos. Cost me a fortune at the photo place.

jokes.text: Serbian rum. So strong it is banned there. Banned in Serbia, Jeff. Let that concept sink in.
jokes.setup: Offering Jeff alcohol
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing how strong alcohol must be to get banned in Serbia, with dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and the 'let that concept sink in' sells the absurdity. Pierce's dramatic delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: This is Serbian rakija. It's so strong, it's been banned in Serbia.

jokes.text: I made that in Photoshop and mailed it to you a month ago so that you'd keep tonight open on your calendar.
jokes.setup: Jeff showing off invitation to exclusive club
jokes.punchline: Revealing the invitation is fake and elaborately planned
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed manipulation - elaborate, technical, and perfectly in character. Great misdirection setup.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Guys, check it out. I got invited to the most exclusive club in the city.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's amazing, Jeff. How did you manage that?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know, right? I must have finally made it.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: There's just one thing. That invitation? I made it. On my computer. Last night. As a prank.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The club doesn't exist. I've been planning this for three weeks.

jokes.text: There's no such thing as single malt platinum boobs and billiards club?
jokes.setup: Learning his invitation is fake
jokes.punchline: Repeating the ridiculous club name with dawning realization
jokes.explanation: The absurd club name is funny when said aloud. Jeff's realization moment works as character beat.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, you're telling me I got invited to the 'Competitive Tickling and Interpretive Screaming Club'?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Competitive... Tickling... and... Interpretive... Screaming...

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This is fake, isn't it?

jokes.text: I guess I never said it out loud.
jokes.setup: Realizing he fell for an obvious fake invitation
jokes.punchline: Admitting he never questioned the ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Jeff's ego making him susceptible to obvious flattery. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I can't believe I fell for that. A guy named 'Buddy Sparkles' sends me an invitation and I just... I never even questioned it.

jokes.text: Don't say charades. Troy & Abed: Yahtzee! Is charades off the table?
jokes.setup: Building up to revealing the night's activity
jokes.punchline: Expecting something exciting but getting board games, then asking for the rejected option
jokes.explanation: Deflating expectations and then asking for the even less exciting option. Solid structure.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So what are we doing tonight? Something awesome, right?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We're playing board games.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Board games? I thought we were going to do something cool!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Board games are cool.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Can we just go with your original plan instead?

jokes.text: Call it Yahtzee all you want. Everybody knows it's Puerto Rican chess.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Yahtzee game
jokes.punchline: Pierce's nonsensical alternative name for the game
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce - confidently stating something completely wrong. The specificity of 'Puerto Rican chess' helps.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know what I call Yahtzee? Dice-a-Rooney.

jokes.text: Just so you know, Jeff, you are now creating six different timelines. Of course I am, Abed.
jokes.setup: Jeff about to roll die to determine who gets pizza
jokes.punchline: Abed's meta-commentary about alternate realities, Jeff's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Sets up the entire episode's concept. Perfect Abed meta-awareness vs Jeff's dismissal. High rewatch value.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: In an alternate timeline, you rolled a one and got no pizza. In another, you rolled a six and got all the pizza. In infinite other timelines, infinite yous are rolling infinite dice.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Abed, I'm just trying to order lunch.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's what they all said.

jokes.text: All this talk of going down... Did you guys know I had sex with Eartha Kitt in an airplane bathroom?
jokes.setup: Annie saying she's 'going down' to get pizza
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inappropriate sexual association and random celebrity story
jokes.explanation: Pure Pierce - inappropriate timing and unverifiable celebrity story. Sets up running gag.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa, 'going down'? That's what she said!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Reminds me of the time I was with Eartha Kitt in an airplane bathroom.

jokes.text: It's not namedropping if it comes up organically.
jokes.setup: Just told random Eartha Kitt story
jokes.punchline: Claiming it was natural when it clearly wasn't
jokes.explanation: Pierce's complete lack of self-awareness. The obvious irony works well.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It was totally natural.

jokes.text: What does a pregnancy test look like? Like a thin piece of plastic with a thing on the end of it. Okay, so this is definitely a gun.
jokes.setup: Troy asking about pregnancy test appearance
jokes.punchline: Using the description to conclude he's found a gun instead
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - logical thinking leading to wrong conclusion. Good escalation from pregnancy to weapon.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What does a pregnancy test look like?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Two pink lines.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh thank God, I found a gun.

jokes.text: That's not a gun, that's a girl's peashooter.
jokes.setup: Everyone worried about gun
jokes.punchline: Pierce dismissing it as inadequate weaponry
jokes.explanation: Pierce's casual attitude toward weapons. Character-specific but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: A gun? That's it? I've fought in three wars. I once took down a man with a shoe.

jokes.text: I don't know, people hitting their heads.
jokes.setup: Asked what's so funny about Jeff hitting his head
jokes.punchline: Simple, honest explanation of physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent honesty about finding physical pain funny. Simple but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: A man hits his head. It's funny because it hurts and he wasn't expecting it. That's physical comedy in its purest form.

jokes.text: ♪Pizza, pizza, go in tummy ♪ ♪me so hung-ee me so hung-ee ♪
jokes.setup: Pizza delivery arrives
jokes.punchline: Troy's childish food song
jokes.explanation: Pure Troy childishness. The broken English 'me so hung-ee' adds to the infantile nature.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Pizza, pizza, in my belly. I love pizza, it's so jelly. Pizza, pizza, on my plate. Pizza makes my taste buds celebrate!

jokes.text: That pizza guy was super creepy. So you're saying he was a pizza guy?
jokes.setup: Britta commenting on pizza delivery guy
jokes.punchline: Abed questioning the most basic assumption
jokes.explanation: Abed's hyper-analytical mind questioning obvious reality. Sets up later timeline reveals.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: He's cute. I wonder if he delivers more than just pizza, if you know what I mean.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I don't know what you mean. Are you implying he also delivers other food items, or are you making a sexual innuendo?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: The second one.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Why would a pizza delivery person have access to other people's bodies?

jokes.text: I wonder what happened in those other timelines.
jokes.setup: Episode establishing timeline concept
jokes.punchline: Abed wondering about alternatives, setting up the episode structure
jokes.explanation: The line that launches the entire episode structure. Perfect Abed meta-commentary that becomes the show's framework.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: What if we're not in the prime timeline? What if there's another version of us somewhere making different choices right now?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: In one timeline, I'm saying this. In another, I'm not. In another, I'm saying something completely different.

jokes.text: The woman has a baking problem.
jokes.setup: Discussing Shirley's compulsive baking
jokes.punchline: Treating baking like an addiction
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay treating hobby like addiction. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Shirley, we're worried about you. This baking thing has gotten out of hand.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Yeah, you're baking at all hours. It's like a compulsion.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: It is NOT a compulsion! I just love baking!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You've baked 47 casseroles this week. That's a compulsion.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Well maybe I'm just passionate!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Honey, I've seen addiction. My first three marriages were addictions. This is addiction.

jokes.text: Heh, you know who got it in the long run. Eartha Kitt, when I nailed her in the airplane bathroom.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning 'long run'
jokes.punchline: Pierce forcing another Eartha Kitt story
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation. Pierce's compulsive storytelling becoming predictable but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That reminds me of the time I met Eartha Kitt in the bathroom of a department store.

jokes.text: What? Came up organically.
jokes.setup: Group's disgusted reaction to inappropriate story
jokes.punchline: Repeating his earlier defense that's even less believable now
jokes.explanation: Good callback that's even more obviously false this time. Pierce's total lack of self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What? I was just trying to be helpful!

jokes.text: It's a traditional Norwegian troll. It used to watch me sleep!
jokes.setup: Pierce giving Troy a 'gift' he knows Troy hates
jokes.punchline: Troy's traumatic memory of the creepy troll
jokes.explanation: Great setup for Pierce's pettiness and Troy's genuine trauma. The escalation to 'watch me sleep' sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I got you something special, Troy. A troll doll. I remember you mentioning how much you loved them.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Pierce, no. No, no, no. That's not... I never said I loved trolls. I said I was terrified of them as a child!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, consider this exposure therapy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's not how exposure therapy works! And that thing... look at its eyes. It's staring into my soul. This is exactly like the one from my neighbor's house. The one that gave me nightmares for three years.

jokes.text: You're not allowed to have 'baking things' as an identity.
jokes.setup: Shirley's breakdown about her pies burning
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment that baking isn't a personality
jokes.explanation: Harsh but true observation about Shirley's limited identity. The air quotes around 'baking things' help.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: My pies! They're ruined!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Shirley, baking isn't a personality.

jokes.text: Excuse me for being the only married woman in a group full of horny toads who sit around all night just making googly eyes at each other.
jokes.setup: Shirley defending her baking identity
jokes.punchline: Attacking everyone else as sexually frustrated children
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley - projecting moral superiority while being petty. 'Horny toads' is a nice touch.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know what? I am a baker. A real baker. And you people are just sexually frustrated children who can't handle a woman with actual skills and confidence.

jokes.text: Nobody makes googly eyes at me either. We're the same.
jokes.setup: Shirley complaining about being left out romantically
jokes.punchline: Pierce trying to bond over mutual undesirability
jokes.explanation: Pierce's attempt at comfort that makes things worse. The earnestness makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Nobody ever asks me out. I'm invisible to men.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Hey, I know how you feel. Women won't even look at me anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yeah. I'm thinking of getting one of those vests that says 'I'm not a threat' on it.

jokes.text: More like a nervous-- Bakedown! I know! I didn't say it on purpose.
jokes.setup: Someone saying Shirley had a nervous breakdown
jokes.punchline: Abed almost making a pun, then catching himself and denying intent
jokes.explanation: Abed's brain making connections he doesn't want to make. Meta-awareness of his own punning tendency.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: She had a nervous break—I mean, she broke down nervously. I wasn't making a pun.

jokes.text: Speaking of crap, I was taking one in an airplane bathroom when Eartha Kitt decided to bang me.
jokes.setup: Someone saying 'crap'
jokes.punchline: Pierce's most graphic and impossible Eartha Kitt story yet
jokes.explanation: Grossest version of the running gag. Escalates the impossibility but becomes predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh, that reminds me of the time I was with Eartha Kitt in an airplane bathroom. I got to say 'crap' one time.

jokes.text: Your mind went years ago.
jokes.setup: Pierce saying 'It's where my mind went'
jokes.punchline: Suggesting his mind is completely gone
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective comeback. Good economy and timing but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It's where my mind went.

dialogue.speaker: Other Character
dialogue.line: Your mind is completely gone.

jokes.text: I can't believe those are real mahogany bunk beds in there. I bet that cost you a few allowances.
jokes.setup: Britta praising Troy's apartment
jokes.punchline: Treating grown Troy like a child getting allowance money
jokes.explanation: Britta undermining Troy while trying to compliment him. The allowance comment is condescending.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: This is a really nice apartment, Troy. You should be so proud of yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Thanks, Britta. It means a lot coming from you.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I mean, look at you, paying bills, keeping plants alive... here, you've earned this.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Is that... is that a gold star sticker?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You've been such a good boy this month!

jokes.text: Um, I was smoking a cigarette. I know what you were doing. I'm 21 years old. I'm a man.
jokes.setup: Troy catching Britta in bathroom doing something embarrassing
jokes.punchline: Troy asserting his maturity while Britta makes obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Troy asserting manhood. Implies Britta was doing something else entirely.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Britta? What are you doing in here?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Nothing! I was just... washing my hands.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Your hands are dry.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm very efficient.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Look, I'm mature enough to handle whatever this is.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I was practicing my acceptance speech for a Peabody Award.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You were singing Beyoncé in the mirror again, weren't you?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: You've got a... bowl full of olives next to the toilet. It's a fancy party, Britta.
jokes.setup: Britta listing Troy's adult accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Toilet olives as evidence of sophistication
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd detail that's exactly what Troy would think is classy. Great character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You've got your own apartment, you pay your own bills, you even have little olives in your bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Those are for my toilet.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I know what they're for, Troy. That's fancy.

jokes.text: He keeps his toiletries in a safe under the sink.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jeff's bathroom habits
jokes.punchline: Revealing Jeff's extreme privacy/security measures
jokes.explanation: Great detail about Jeff's paranoid personality. Specific and believable character insight.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Jeff, why do you need three locks on the bathroom door?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's called privacy, Troy. Some of us value our personal space.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Three locks? That's not privacy, that's a panic room. What are you doing in there, defusing a bomb?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I also installed a keypad. And security cameras.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Security cameras? In the bathroom?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Only pointing inward. For my own records.

jokes.text: Man, pizza guys are getting worse and worse-looking. Guess all the good ones went into porn.
jokes.setup: Commenting on pizza delivery guy's appearance
jokes.punchline: Suggesting attractive pizza guys become porn actors
jokes.explanation: Pierce's inappropriate observation that's oddly logical. Dark humor that fits his character.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know, with looks like that, you should be doing porn, not delivering pizzas.

jokes.text: You don't have to treat me like a kid anymore, remember? Yeah, but adults still need to be protected. I-I can't help but worry about you, Annie. You're important to me.
jokes.setup: Annie asserting her adulthood after mentioning stabbing
jokes.punchline: Jeff's protective instincts revealing deeper feelings
jokes.explanation: Character development moment with romantic undertones. More drama than pure comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm an adult. I can stab whoever I want.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, you can't.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Because I said so.

jokes.text: Feel the terror! Stop the tantrum! Feel the terror of the Norwegian troll!
jokes.setup: Pierce terrorizing Troy with the troll doll
jokes.punchline: Dramatic proclamations while chasing Troy
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation. Pierce's theatrical evil proclamations add to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You can't escape me, Troy! I am the master of this troll doll, and you are its prey!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Pierce, please! Just put it down!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Never! The troll has spoken, and its hunger knows no bounds!

jokes.text: Hey, guys, this is Toby, our pizza delivery guy. And I know this is gonna sound nuts, but we're in love and we're getting married.
jokes.setup: Timeline where Britta went to get pizza
jokes.punchline: Instant engagement to creepy pizza guy
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Britta's impulsive nature. The casualness of the announcement sells the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I went to get pizza and somehow got instantly engaged to the creepy pizza guy.

jokes.text: Wait, there are other timelines?
jokes.setup: Abed wondering about other timelines
jokes.punchline: Britta suddenly taking the concept seriously
jokes.explanation: Breaking the fourth wall - character becoming aware of episode structure. Meta-humor that works.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I wonder what I'm doing in other timelines. Like, in one timeline, I'm probably a really successful film director.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: In another timeline, you're probably dead.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Thanks, Britta.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, I'm serious. You should be careful. There are infinite versions of you, and statistically, most of them are probably dead.

jokes.text: ♪You don't have to put on the red light ♪ Water. Water! Water! Breathe, breathe. Nooooooo!
jokes.setup: Pierce shot himself and apartment is on fire
jokes.punchline: Chaos escalating with Troy singing while Pierce dies
jokes.explanation: Peak chaos timeline - multiple disasters happening simultaneously. Troy's oblivious singing during tragedy is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Troy sings while Pierce is dying in the burning apartment

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Pierce makes final commentary while flames consume the room

jokes.text: Oh, my God. They taste just like regular-size pies.
jokes.setup: Tasting Shirley's mini pies
jokes.punchline: Amazement that small pies taste like big pies
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about obvious fact. Jeff's fake amazement works as character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, these taste like regular pies.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: They're mini pies, Jeff.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know, but they taste like full-size pies. How is that possible?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: It's the same recipe, just smaller portions.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This is witchcraft.

jokes.text: Crap, I was not supposed to say that out loud! Crap, okay, cards on the table, I'm really high right now.
jokes.setup: Jeff accidentally revealing the no-pie pact
jokes.punchline: Escalating admissions culminating in drug confession
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - each admission makes the situation worse. Jeff's loss of control is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, I wasn't supposed to mention the no-pie pact.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: What no-pie pact?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. We also agreed never to eat ice cream on Tuesdays.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And we may have made a blood oath about pudding.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're spiraling.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: There's also the thing about the methamphetamine lab in the study room!

dialogue.speaker: Everyone
dialogue.line: WHAT?!

jokes.text: A waterpik! A speedboat! Two waterpiks.
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to give Jeff gifts while high
jokes.punchline: Increasingly random and impractical gift offers
jokes.explanation: Pierce's stoned generosity escalating absurdly. The specificity of dental equipment and boats is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Jeff, my boy, I want to give you something special. How about a lifetime supply of turtle necks?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: No? Okay, okay, I've got it - a jet ski. A jet ski for the library.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Still no? Fine, fine, I'm thinking bigger now. What about my collection of vintage pornographic playing cards?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: A solid gold toilet seat with your initials engraved in it?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: My old toupee collection? I've got seventeen of them, Jeff. Seventeen!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What if I just... gave you a year's supply of expired mayonnaise? I've got it in the freezer.

jokes.text: Drug addict? You're a pie pusher! You push pies to get love.
jokes.setup: Shirley calling Jeff a drug addict
jokes.punchline: Jeff turning baking into drug dealing metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal - making baking sound like drug dealing. 'Pie pusher' is a great coinage.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You're a drug addict, Jeff.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not a drug addict. I'm a baker. I just sold some brownies to a guy behind the library for fifty bucks.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: He said they were 'life-changing.' I'm thinking of expanding my operation.

jokes.text: Stop. A little Make Out 101. Less dad talk. And, uh, you could ease up on the bubblegum lip gloss.
jokes.setup: Annie and Jeff having a moment
jokes.punchline: Britta giving unsolicited kissing advice with specific critiques
jokes.explanation: Britta's awkward interruption with specific criticisms. The bubblegum lip gloss detail is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Okay, so I'm watching you two, and Jeff, your technique is very textbook. Very... by-the-book. Annie, you're doing that thing where you're thinking too hard. I can see it happening. You're literally thinking during the kiss. Also, Jeff, your jaw is too tense. Relax your jaw.

jokes.text: Give me this, you godless hippie skank! You're a sick, sad, twisted old man. And I hope you die alone.
jokes.setup: Fight over something escalating
jokes.punchline: Increasingly vicious personal attacks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Shirley to Pierce. The contrast between sweet Shirley and vicious insults works.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know what, Pierce? You're a terrible person!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh, that's rich coming from you, you sanctimonious hypocrite!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: At least I don't spend all day making racist comments and thinking I'm hilarious!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: And at least I don't hide behind religion to judge everyone while being just as awful!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You're a fossil! A walking, talking relic from an era nobody misses!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You're a shrill, obnoxious busybody who nobody actually likes!

jokes.text: I gotta say, I hope this is the real one, because I just found a nickel in the hallway.
jokes.setup: Abed wondering about timelines
jokes.punchline: Troy's simple happiness about finding money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy - completely missing the point about reality vs. being happy about a nickel. Pure character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I wonder what happened in the timeline where I found twenty dollars.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What? I found twenty dollars!

jokes.text: The universe is an endless raging sea of randomness. Our job isn't to fight it, but to weather it--together. On the raft of life. A raft held together by those few rare, beautiful things that we know to be predictable.
jokes.setup: Preventing Jeff from rolling the die
jokes.punchline: Abed giving an unexpectedly profound speech about chaos and friendship
jokes.explanation: Abed's most philosophical moment - using chaos theory to talk about friendship. Beautiful character growth.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You can't roll the die, Jeff. Not because of the rules, but because chaos isn't something you control—it's something you accept. And right now, the only thing more chaotic than a die roll is a group of people who've learned to care about each other despite everything telling them not to. That's the real roll. That's what matters.

jokes.text: There are six sides to this die and seven of us. He devised a system by which he never has to get the pizza.
jokes.setup: Abed's beautiful speech about friendship and predictability
jokes.punchline: Revealing Jeff's selfish mathematical manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Abed's speech seemed about chaos/friendship but was actually exposing Jeff's scheme. Brilliant structure.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You know what I love about our group? We're predictable. In the best way. Jeff does something selfish, we call him out, he has a moment of growth, and we all hug it out. It's beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, what? I calculated every variable in that equation to make sure I'd come out on top.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Exactly. Predictable.

jokes.text: It's called friendship, look it up. Encarta it.
jokes.setup: Being called out for manipulating his friends
jokes.punchline: Defending selfishness as friendship with outdated reference
jokes.explanation: Jeff's shameless defense and the outdated 'Encarta it' reference. Good character moment showing his age.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not manipulating you guys. I'm just... strategically leveraging our friendship for mutual benefit.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And honestly, that's what friendship is about. It's like that song says: 'You scratch my back, I'll scratch yours.' Classic friendship anthem.

jokes.text: That guy sucks harder than the toilet in an airplane bathroom. Airplane bathroom...
jokes.setup: Insulting Jeff with airplane bathroom comparison
jokes.punchline: Pierce getting triggered into another Eartha Kitt memory
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Pierce's callback. The pause before 'Airplane bathroom...' sells his mental trigger.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What's wrong with you? You're like an airplane bathroom—small, cramped, and full of crap.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's it! That reminds me of the time I was in an airplane bathroom with Eartha Kitt!

jokes.text: ♪Roxanne ♪ ♪you don't have to put on the red light ♪
jokes.setup: Happy timeline where everyone gets along
jokes.punchline: Group singing and dancing together harmoniously
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the perfect timeline - everyone happy and musical. Contrast with chaos timelines is effective.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This is actually pretty great, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I know, right? No conflicts, no drama.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We should do this more often!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: This is like a musical montage sequence.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Everyone singing together in perfect harmony!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Hallelujah! This is what I've been praying for!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Even I'm not ruining this with something inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Group
dialogue.line: *singing and dancing together*

jokes.text: This is the darkest, most terrible timeline.
jokes.setup: Everyone's lives have gone wrong after Pierce's death
jokes.punchline: Abed's meta-commentary on their reality
jokes.explanation: Iconic line that became a meme. Abed's meta-awareness of their fictional situation is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We're like a TV show that just lost its weirdest character. Now we're just a standard ensemble drama waiting to get cancelled.

jokes.text: Troy lost his larynx because, for some dumb reason, he tried to destroy a flaming troll doll by eating it.
jokes.setup: Explaining what went wrong in their timeline
jokes.punchline: Troy's impossibly stupid solution to fire problem
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Troy logic - eating fire to stop it. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: In this timeline, there was a fire in the blanket fort.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why don't we just put it out with more fire?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's not how fire works, Troy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not a scientist!

jokes.text: Clearly you don't understand anything about defeating trolls.
jokes.setup: Abed criticizing Troy's troll-eating strategy
jokes.punchline: Troy defending his method as if trolls were real
jokes.explanation: Troy's complete commitment to fantasy logic. Even mute, he's defending his troll expertise.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You can't just eat a troll like that. You need to think strategically.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? This is exactly how you eat a troll. You go in fast, catch it off guard, and—

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's not how this works.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Look, I've done this before, okay? The key is you want to get the troll when it's vulnerable. If you hesitate, it's going to turn on you.

jokes.text: Britta, you put one wash-away blue streak in your hair. And I lost an arm.
jokes.setup: Discussing how dark their lives have become
jokes.punchline: Contrasting minor hair change with major amputation
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Britta's minor rebellion and Jeff's major injury. Scale humor works well.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Our lives have gotten pretty dark.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, I dyed my hair black.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I lost my arm.

jokes.text: I made us all black goatees out of felt. I suggest you put them on until you're able to grow your own.
jokes.setup: Abed proposing they become evil versions
jokes.punchline: Practical evil accessories with growing instructions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed solution - practical approach to being evil. The growing instructions are delightfully specific.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We could become evil versions of ourselves.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: How? Evil goatees?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We'd need practical evil accessories. I'm thinking eye patches with growing instructions.

jokes.text: I hate you! Shut up with your Sci-Fi crap! I lost my damn arm, and you're making fake beards! Goatees.
jokes.setup: Jeff exploding at Abed's evil plan
jokes.punchline: Abed correcting terminology while being yelled at
jokes.explanation: Abed's pedantic correction in the middle of Jeff's breakdown. Perfect character priority - accuracy over empathy.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You can't just manipulate people like that! Do you have any idea how evil that is?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Actually, it's not evil. It's morally gray.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's EVIL, Abed!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Evil has a specific definition. This is more of a chaotic neutral situation.

jokes.text: ♪evil Troy and evil Abed ♪
jokes.setup: Dark timeline ending
jokes.punchline: Evil version of their signature jingle
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend - evil version of their opening theme. Great callback with sinister twist.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Hey Abed, you know what this calls for?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Our jingle?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Our jingle.

dialogue.speaker: Troy and Abed
dialogue.line: Troy and Abed in the moooorning! (Evil laugh) In the morning!

tmdb_overview: When Jeff introduces an element of randomness into Troy and Abed's housewarming party, reality splits into six timelines.
insight: Six timelines spawn 65 jokes in 42 minutes — Community's most ambitious structural comedy.
